Okay, so this is like a huge video. I'm really excited about this because I've said this a lot of times but I haven't articulated it clearly. So how do you make your offers ever green? Alright, so one of the number of things that I hear from gym owners is like, dude, like we had this thing and it was working for a while, stop working and you know now, now we don't know what to do, right? So usually it's not changing your offer, okay? So that's the first thing. So what things do you need to do in order to keep an offer fresh in a workplace? Like at the end of the day, if you are a gym owner or you're you know, you're running a gym facility, like you're either going to be offering a trial or a challenge. Like that's really what it comes down to. So like it's not like your offer really needs to change that much, okay? LA Fitness has been running in three months since like in the 60s, okay? They have a great business. The point is, it's not, it's rarely the offer that needs to change especially if it's something that you've already had success with. The first thing that you usually need to change is just the creative. What I mean by that is the pictures, the videos, the things that have colors on them that most people see, okay? Now the reason that you change that first is because that's the thing that most people remember. Like most people are very visual and they're going to remember pictures and images and be like, very quickly be able to identify that and be like, oh I've already seen that before, right? So that's the first thing you change. You know, seven out of ten times that's all you need to do. Refresh the campaign, do it again and you're good, okay? Now this is like most times, okay? Now below that, the next thing that you change, and this is like after changing the creative multiple, multiple times, like you've already run like six, seven, eight, nine campaigns with the same offers, headlines, copy. At that point what you would do is you change the copy. Now the offer and the headline still might still be the same, right? You still might be running a 28 day challenge or a six week challenge, whatever, right? But you just change the copy up. So it just, it just refreshes the ad, okay? So you just change some of the benefits, how you articulate some of the things, but it's still going to follow the same structure. It's just the words are going to be different, okay? After you've changed these two things, the next thing you would change would be the headline, okay? So this is like, instead of saying, you know, attention however many men are local, right, you know, whatever, you might change it to, we're looking for 30 local residents or, or attention men with money or whatever it is, right? So you're just going to change the headline of what you're calling out. So that would also be the bold text in an ad if you're running like a Facebook ad, okay? So first thing you change is created most times, all right? Like if you haven't already changed this like six or seven times, just go back to this. Now if you have changed this six to seven times, then at that point you probably tweaked the copy, all right? Teach it up a little bit. All right? Then after that you change the headline. Then the fourth thing and the last thing, the rarest thing that you'll ever change is an offer, especially if it's something that's converted well for you in the past. Because ultimately, like the way that you service a customer, the way that a customer is introduced to your business is more or less going to be the same thing. Like for us, right? Like we offer a case study to a strategy call. Like that's more or less what we're going to be doing for a really long time. But the ads, the copy and the creative are going to be the things that change a lot. But these things not nearly as much, okay? So how do you make your offers evergreen? They usually are evergreen. You just need to change these two things. I was talking to a buddy of mine, Travis Jones, and we were talking about just Jim, Jim businesses in general. You know, he's like, dude, it's not that freaking hard. I don't get why everyone complicates it. He's like, I've been running a 28 day challenge for four years. That's it. Four straight years. He's been running the same thing. All right. So the thing is, is once you have an offer that converts and you have a process that works, you don't need to change that. You don't need to change your offer. The only thing people need to come into your gym and try it out. Like this is what it is, right? And you have to have a process of upselling them into a membership, right? Providing value. But these are the rarely the things that are going to change once it's converted, right? And just as a second, like a secondary note there, Travis has eight gyms in Melbourne. Like in one city, he has eight locations. So whenever people are like, Hey, there's competitors nearby. What if I, you know, like, you know, I don't want more people running the same types of things as me. Like if you're crushing it in a marketplace, you burn a copy. Guarantee. Like, if they're not, then it means you're not crushing it. It's really what it comes down to. All right. But the thing is, is that you're going to beat them on the fact that you're going to do the work, the change of creative up regularly and change a copy of occasionally. And the things is that like this usually takes an hour, but most people just don't do anything. All right. They just get paralyzed and they put out fires the day they don't actually do any productive work. It sounds crazy, but it's like really true. And then this is really where you need to focus. Okay. So you don't need to worry about your market or your competition or like, am I saturating my like, am I saturating the market? I mean, you should be. But the thing is, is that the people who need to sign up for weight loss today are different than they were two months ago. So it's not a big deal. It's just who's going to raise their hand this month. That's all it comes down to. You need to have your offer in front of them so that they can raise your hand for yours. That's it. And as long as you have a conversion process that's monetizable, that makes sense. They can liquidate your act costs. You get free clients. Right. So these are the four components of any kind of offer from a marketing state, like an advertising standpoint. And this is the order that you would change them and most of the time, like 90% of the time, it's just going to be these two. It's all you got to change up. That's it. These two things, you rarely, rarely, rarely need to change. It's usually these. So put your focus there. That's how you create every green offer is I ran a six week challenge for four and a half years and people in that marketplace still respond to it. Okay. Like you just have to switch it up and be creative. Like that's all it really comes down to. So anyways, hope you're having an awesome day.
